title: 60 percent surveyed professionals say AI now central to HR operations: Report
description: Artificial intelligence is now a top priority for 60% of HR professionals, revolutionizing recruitment, onboarding, and daily operations. The primary drivers for AI adoption are boosting efficiency and productivity, with many reporting significant improvements in speed and seamlessness. AI is also reshaping workforce structures by reducing manual roles and enabling smarter planning, signaling a move towards skill-first organizations.

title: Senator seeking public ownership of AI firms
description: WASHINGTON -- As artificial intelligence companies reshape the economy and race toward trillion-dollar valuations, Sen. Bernie Sanders is proposing a sweeping transfer of wealth and power from the industry to the American public.

title: Trump tried to block states from regulating AI, but some are forging ahead
description: Six months after President Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that.
content:

title: AI spending boom may keep US inflation and interest rates elevated: Jefferies Report
description: The ongoing surge in artificial intelligence (AI)-related spending could keep inflation elevated in the United States and force interest rates to remain higher for longer, according to Jefferies' latest Greed & Fear report.

title: Natural gas key to Alberta’s AI ambitions, Premier says
description: Premier Danielle Smith says natural gas will be key to Alberta’s ambitions of becoming Canada’s artificial intelligence hub, but experts and residents say the strategy raises questions about emissions, electricity demand and how large-scale developments could affect communities.

title: How To Spot the Red Flags Of Artificial Intelligence Scams
description: As cybercriminals increasingly rely on artificial intelligence to build speed, scale, and deeply personalized messages, online scams are becoming far more convincing.

title: AI can improve lives, Nvidia chief says
description: SHERMAN, Texas -- Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang -- whose work helped propel artificial intelligence -- stressed in an Associated Press interview Tuesday that society needs to change with the advent of AI, arguing that a fuller embrace of the technology would improve people's lives.
